Campaign.description:
An orchestrated marketing push: content + SEO + ads lanes running
together against a time window and a content-credit budget.
Campaign.title: Campaign

CampaignItem.description:
One lane in a campaign.

kind='video'   ref_id -> niche  (auto-generate + post to socials)
kind='article' ref_id -> niche  (SEO article cadence)
kind='ad'      ref_id -> ad campaign (linked; governed lifecycle)
CampaignItem.title: CampaignItem

Composition.description: A remix: a new video assembled from existing library clips.
Composition.title: Composition

CreditTransaction.description: One movement of prepaid credit — purchase, grant, or pipeline debit.
CreditTransaction.title: CreditTransaction

Kit.description:
A user-level reusable skill injected into agent runtimes.

kind='design'  -> video direction (scriptwriter + visual director)
kind='writing' -> article pipeline voice/style
kind='ad'      -> ads optimization proposer (propose-only; the
                  fail-closed spend guard is never relaxed by a kit)
Kit.title: Kit

MediaAsset.description:
One indexed media artifact: a scene clip, keyframe, voiceover,
final video, or a remixed composition output.
MediaAsset.title: MediaAsset

NicheUpdate.description:
All fields optional — partial update.

Mirrors :class:`NicheCreate` so the web client can POST the same
payload to either endpoint and the backend interprets unset keys
as "leave alone".
NicheUpdate.title: NicheUpdate

PersonalAccessToken.description: Public-safe view of a PAT row. Never contains the hash.
PersonalAccessToken.title: PersonalAccessToken

PromptOverrides.description:
Verbatim extra instructions appended to one agent's prompt each.

Bounded so a runaway paste can't blow the context (or the token bill).
PromptOverrides.title: PromptOverrides

motion_prompt.description: Grok Imagine prompt describing the animation
motion_prompt.title: Motion Prompt

narration.description: What the VO says during this scene
narration.title: Narration

Template.description:
Admin-curated remixable reference: an asset + the exact prompt that
produced its look. Users remix with their own product image and the
generation inherits the aesthetic.
Template.title: Template

TodaySpend.description: Mirrors backend.routes.spend.TodaySpend so SDK callers have one shape.
TodaySpend.title: TodaySpend

UserSettingsUpdate.description:
Body for PATCH /api/v1/users/me — all fields optional (only the keys
present are changed).
UserSettingsUpdate.title: UserSettingsUpdate

BearerAuth.description: Personal access token issued via /api/v1/tokens. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

info.description: Programmatic access to marketer: campaigns, articles, ads, billing, and publishing automation. Authenticate with a personal access token (PAT) as a Bearer token. Errors are returned as a stable JSON envelope (see backend.errors) so agent/SDK callers can branch on `error.code`.
info.title: marketer API

Response List Flags Api V1 Admin Flags Get

get.description:
Operational snapshot: DB reachability + recent failure/skew signals.
Read-only; audited as a view.

schema.title: Response System Health Api V1 Admin Health Get
Response List Users Api V1 Admin Users Get
Response Grant Credits Api V1 Admin Users  User Id  Credits Post
Response List Accounts Api V1 Ads Accounts Get
Response Connect Account Api V1 Ads Accounts Connect Post
Response List Actions Api V1 Ads Actions Get
Response List Approvals Api V1 Ads Approvals Get

post.description:
Decide a pending approval. Approving does not just flip the row to
'approved' — it also EXECUTES the underlying spend change (budget
change or campaign activation) through the safe-execute layer, so a
human's "yes" actually takes effect instead of dead-ending. Rejecting
never executes anything. If the re-guard at execution time denies (state
moved since the approval was granted), the approval row stays 'approved'
for a later retry and we surface a 402 with the reason — we never crash
or silently drop the approved-but-unexecuted change.

schema.title: Response List Campaigns Api V1 Ads Campaigns Get

post.description:
Create a DRAFT campaign. No spend — drafts are never on a platform until
activated. The account must belong to the caller.

schema.title: Response Get Campaign Api V1 Ads Campaigns  Campaign Id  Get

post.description:
Change a campaign's daily budget through the safe-execute layer:
guarded, approval-gated for large deltas, and audited. 402 on a hard deny.

schema.title: Response Change Budget Api V1 Ads Campaigns  Campaign Id  Budget Post

post.description:
Activate / pause / end a campaign. Pausing/ending always allowed.
Activation is spend-affecting: it must pass BOTH the guard's non-budget
check (account status / kill-switch) AND the same budget guard
``change_budget`` uses, evaluated against the campaign's own
``daily_budget_usd`` against the account's caps — otherwise a large
draft budget could go live with zero cap check and zero approval. A
denial returns 402; a delta over the approval threshold parks an
approval and does NOT activate until it is approved.

schema.title: Response Change Status Api V1 Ads Campaigns  Campaign Id  Status Post

get.description:
Ads dashboard summary: spend today / 30d, active campaigns, pending
approvals. Cheap read-only aggregation across the user's accounts.

schema.title: Response Overview Api V1 Ads Overview Get
Response List Articles Api V1 Articles Get

post.description:
Create the article row and spawn the Modal pipeline against it.
Poll GET /{article_id} for status.

get.description:
Stream the article's editorial hero image (gpt-image-1 PNG).

Ownership-scoped like every other media endpoint. 404 if the article is
missing/foreign, has no hero, or the file isn't on the volume.

post.description: Re-run a failed article from scratch (same row, same topic).

Repurpose a finished article into platform-native social posts. One
metered LLM call (charged to the article's niche daily cap). The article
must be done and have content.

schema.title: Response Repurpose To Social Api V1 Articles  Article Id  Social Post

post.description: Stripe event receiver — no bearer auth; signature-verified.

schema.title: Response Stripe Webhook Api V1 Billing Webhook Post

get.description: Return the user's brand kit, or an empty kit if none is set yet.

Scheduled videos + article activity in a window. Defaults to the next
`days` from now; pass explicit start/end to page any range.

schema.title: Response Calendar Api V1 Calendar Get
Response List Campaigns Api V1 Campaigns Get

get.description:
The caller's recent failures across jobs, image posts, and
articles, newest first, each with a triage category attached.

``limit`` bounds *each* underlying query, not the merged total —
keeping every source's most-recent window cheap and independent
rather than paginating a UNION.

post.description:
Replay a single failed item by delegating to the same repo call
and Modal function the item's own retry endpoint uses. No new spawn
logic is introduced here — this is the existing per-surface retry,
just reachable from the consolidated inbox.

schema.title: Response Replay Failure Api V1 Failures Replay  Kind   Item Id  Post
Response List Image Posts Api V1 Image Posts Get
Response Enqueue Image Post Api V1 Image Posts Post
Response Get Image Post Api V1 Image Posts  Image Post Id  Get

post.description: Operator sign-off: atomically claim and resume at scheduling.

schema.title: Response Approve Image Post Api V1 Image Posts  Image Post Id  Approve Post

post.description: Re-run a failed post from the top (fresh plan + renders).

schema.title: Response Retry Image Post Api V1 Image Posts  Image Post Id  Retry Post
Response List Jobs Api V1 Jobs Get

post.description:
Spawn a pipeline run on Modal. Returns the queued Job row;
poll GET /{job_id} for status.

Operator sign-off on an `awaiting_approval` job. Spawns the Modal
`finish_scheduling` function, which uploads + schedules the already
rendered video and marks the job done.

get.description:
Return the latest analytics sample and full time-series history for a job.

Auth-scoped: the job must belong to the requesting user. Returns 404 if the
job does not exist or is owned by another user.  Returns ``{"latest": null,
"history": []}`` for jobs that have never been sampled yet.

post.description:
Operator veto on an `awaiting_approval` job. The rendered video
stays on the volume (retention GC handles cleanup); the job is marked
failed so it never posts.

Re-run a previously failed job from scratch. Only works on jobs in
`failed` state owned by the caller.

get.description:
Stream the rendered mp4 for a finished job.

Ownership is checked (the job must belong to ``ctx.user_id``). Any
of "job missing", "no rendered video on the job", or "file missing
on disk" produce a 404 — the web client doesn't differentiate.

schema.title: Response List Kits Api V1 Kits Get
Response List Assets Api V1 Library Get
Response List Compositions Api V1 Library Compositions Get

post.description: Validate the clips, persist the composition, spawn the render.

get.description:
Playback/download for one asset.

Wasabi-stored assets redirect to a short-lived presigned URL (the
bytes stream straight from object storage, not through the API).
Volume-stored assets stream from the artifacts volume.

schema.title: Response List Niches Api V1 Niches Get

post.description:
One sentence in, a full channel spec out. The onboarding front
door: the client shows the returned fields on a review screen so the
user launches instead of filling a 16-field form.

schema.title: Response Draft Niche Spec Api V1 Niches Draft Post

put.description: Partial update. Fields left unset are not touched.

get.description:
The niche's generated character sheet — the face of the channel.

404 until the first pipeline run generates it; the niche detail page
hides the card in that case.

Return performance attribution for all jobs in the niche over `days`.

Steps:
1. Verify niche ownership (404 if missing or not owned).
2. List jobs for the niche created in the window.
3. Batch-fetch spend and latest metrics in two round-trips.
4. Build JobPerformance entries, then compute summary stats.

Config-health report (Cycle-2 Team 3's preflight helper), surfaced
here so an operator can see "misconfigured integration" alongside
"stuck jobs" and "provider errors" in one place.

Imported defensively: if `marketer.services.preflight` isn't present in
this checkout yet (or its shape changes), this degrades to an explicit
`available: false` rather than 500ing the whole ops page.

schema.title: Response Ops Config Health Api V1 Ops Config Health Get

get.description:
Real-time ops snapshot: spend velocity (1h/24h by provider), provider
error rates over `error_window_minutes`, and stuck-work counts using
`stuck_after_minutes` as the reap threshold. Every number is a live
aggregate over `spend_ledger`/`jobs`/`image_posts` — nothing here is
sampled or fabricated.

schema.title: Response List Script Models Api V1 Providers Script Models Get
Response List Video Models Api V1 Providers Video Models Get

get.description:
Return per-day spend aggregates for the authenticated user.

``days`` must be between 1 and 90 (inclusive). ``niche_id`` narrows
the result to a single niche. ``group_by`` is reserved for future
expansion (only ``"day"`` is supported now).

Response List Tokens Api V1 Tokens Get

delete.description:
GDPR right to erasure: permanently delete the account and all its data
(niches, jobs, articles, spend history, tokens) via FK cascade. This is
irreversible; the frontend must confirm before calling.

patch.description:
Update mutable user settings.

Supports ``global_daily_cap_usd`` (pass ``null`` to clear; negatives are
rejected with 422) and ``email_notifications`` (opt out of terminal-state
emails). All fields optional — only keys present in the request are
changed.

get.description:
GDPR data portability: download everything we hold about you as JSON.
Personal access tokens are exported by prefix only (never the secret).

schema.title: Response List Endpoints Api V1 Webhook Endpoints Get

post.description:
Register an endpoint. The signing secret is returned exactly once in
this response (never again) — the client must store it to verify
signatures.

patch.description:
Pause or resume delivery. A disabled endpoint keeps its history and
signing secret; re-enabling resumes with the same secret.

post.description:
Send a signed `test.ping` event so the client can validate their
receiver and signature verification before real events fire.

schema.title: Response Send Test Api V1 Webhook Endpoints  Endpoint Id  Test Post
Response Ayrshare Webhook Api V1 Webhooks Ayrshare Post

get.description:
Discover whether x402 top-ups are available and the accepted network/
asset/bounds — so an agent can decide before attempting a payment.
